questions.original_question: 路特斯科技前三季度生活用车和跑车的交付量分别是多少？
questions.answer: 在2024年的前三个季度，路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）在生活用车和跑车领域的交付量均表现出色。具体来看，生活用车的交付量达到3,994辆，其中第一季度交付了1,041辆，第二季度交付了1,348辆，第三季度交付了1,605辆。与此同时，跑车的交付量总计为7,617辆，同比增长136%。这些数据充分展示了路特斯科技在全球市场的强劲增长势头，尤其是在生活用车和跑车领域的持续扩展和成功。以上信息基于2024年11月21日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

rag_info.chunk_content:
Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

summary: Lotus Technology has revealed promising preliminary delivery results for 2024, showcasing a notable rise in vehicle deliveries driven by robust demand for its electric vehicles. The company is focused on maintaining this growth trajectory and enhancing its market presence in the near future. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. title: Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

In the first nine months of 2024, the Company achieved significant growth, delivering a total of 7,617 units, representing a remarkable 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Deliveries were well-distributed across various regions, with each contributing between 18% to 35% to the overall total, supported by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally. Additionally, the unveiling of the Eletre Carbon variant for North America is anticipated, with deliveries expected to start in the first half of 2025.

Sales figures for the period included 3,983 units of "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan," reflecting a 126% increase from 1,762 units in 2023, and 3,634 units of "Sportscars," which marked a 149% rise from 1,459 units in the previous year. Regionally, Europe accounted for 2,683 units (35% share) in 2024, a significant increase from 568 units (18% share) in 2023. China recorded 1,880 units (25% share), a slight decline from 1,993 units (62% share) in 2023. North America reported 1,665 units (22% share) with no prior data for comparison, while the Rest of the World achieved 1,389 units (18% share), up from 660 units (20% share) in 2023.

Financially, the Company reported revenue of 19 units for the first nine months of 2024, representing a substantial 217% YoY increase from 6 units in 2023. Revenue from Lotus increased to 8 units, reflecting a 100% rise, while revenue from other customers surged to 11 units, a remarkable 450% increase from 2 units in 2023. Furthermore, Lotus Robotics secured contracts valued at approximately $130 million for intelligent driving solutions with various automakers, significantly enhancing its technological footprint within the industry.

Overall, the first nine months of 2024 demonstrated the Company's robust growth trajectory, marked by increased deliveries, expanded market presence, and substantial revenue growth, positioning it favorably for future developments.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.
